core.readers.sensor_reader module

SENSOR READER - Temperature and Voltage Sensors from Sysfs

This module reads temperature and voltage sensors from the Linux sysfs interface. Unlike turbostat which reads CPU-internal sensors, this reads motherboard and package sensors exposed by kernel drivers.

Requirements implemented in this module: - Req 1.9: Package thermal jitter (temperature monitoring) - Req 1.2: Motherboard voltages (voltage monitoring) - Req 1.38: System Agent voltage (VSA monitoring)

Sources: - CPU package temperature: /sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone*/temp - Core temperatures: Multiple thermal zones - Voltages: /sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/in*_input (if available)

Why this matters for AI energy research: - Thermal throttling detection (Req 1.9) - Correlation between temperature and energy consumption - Validation that cooling is sufficient between runs

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.readers.sensor_reader.SensorReader(config: Dict)[source]

Bases: object

Reads temperature and voltage sensors from sysfs.

This class implements: - Req 1.9: Package thermal jitter (temperature monitoring) - Req 1.2: Motherboard voltages (voltage monitoring) - Req 1.38: System Agent voltage (VSA monitoring) from core.utils.debug import dprint All paths are read from hw_config.json, which is generated by detect_hardware.py during system detection.

close()[source]

Close all open thermal file handles.

initialize()[source]

Initialize thermal sampling - call after config is fully loaded

is_throttling(threshold: float = 85.0) → bool[source]

Quick check if CPU is thermal throttling.

Req 1.9: Thermal throttling detection - If temperature exceeds threshold, measurements may be unreliable due to frequency reduction.

Parameters:

threshold – Temperature threshold in Celsius (default 85°C)

Returns:

True if package temperature exceeds threshold

read_all_thermal() → Dict[str, float | None][source]

Read all configured thermal sensors.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping role -> temperature in °C (or None if failed)

read_temperatures() → ThermalReadings[source]

Read all temperature sensors and return ThermalReadings object.

Req 1.9: Package thermal jitter - Reads CPU package temperature to detect thermal throttling and ensure measurement validity.

Returns:

  • package_temperature_celsius

  • core_temperatures_celsius (list)

  • Any other available thermal data

Return type:

ThermalReadings object containing

read_throttle_events() → int[source]

Read thermal throttle events from MSR or sysfs.

Req 1.9: Thermal throttling events - Counts how many times the CPU has throttled due to high temperature.

This is a simplified version – full throttle detection requires MSR access which is handled by msr_reader.py.

Returns:

Number of throttle events (0 if not available)

read_voltages() → Dict[str, float][source]

Read voltage sensors from hwmon interface (if available).

Req 1.2: Motherboard voltages - Reads voltage rails to detect if the board over-volts during rapid state transitions.

Req 1.38: System Agent voltage - Specifically looks for VSA to detect if SoC keeps interconnect “hot” during idle.

This is optional and may not be available on all systems.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping voltage names to values in volts. Example: {‘Vcore’: 1.2, ‘VSA’: 1.05}
